title: XPeng Q2 2023 Earnings: Delivery Growth Despite Margin Pressure
text: XPeng delivered 23,205 cars in the second quarter of 2023, logging a 27% quarter-on-quarter rise and beating its own forecast. In terms of company outlook, XPeng aimed to break even in 2025 according to statements made during their earnings call. The stock surged more than 70% year-to-date and 173% over the past 12 months, supported by robust vehicle deliveries, rising revenue, and new model launches. The company had outperformed consensus EPS estimates in six of its last eight quarters, keeping investor expectations elevated heading into this release. The earnings showed solid delivery momentum and beat-the-forecast results, though the company continued to navigate profitability challenges.
